content: Which one of the following best describes peptones?

options.content: An intestinal mixture of proteins, mucous and $\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}$

explanation:
Peptones are best described by Option B: partially digested proteins

• They’re the water-soluble protein fragments formed when enzymes (like pepsin) hydrolyze larger proteins

• Not a zymogen (that’d be pepsinogen) nor the active enzyme itself

• They’re simply intermediate digestion products, not a mixture of mucous or bicarbonate

content: Which of the following products are expected when lipase acts on diglycerides?
explanation:
When lipase catalyzes the hydrolysis of acylglycerol molecules, it breaks the ester bonds linking fatty acids to the glycerol backbone. A diglyceride consists of a glycerol molecule attached to two fatty acids. When both ester bonds are hydrolyzed, the reaction is:

$$ \text{Diglyceride} + 2H_2O \stackrel{\text{lipase}}{\longrightarrow} \text{Glycerol} + 2\ \text{Fatty Acids} $$

Key points to consider:

Lipase is an enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis (splitting by water) of ester bonds.

In the case of a diglyceride (glycerol with two fatty acids attached), complete hydrolysis removes both fatty acids.

The final products of the reaction are free fatty acids and glycerol.

Among the options provided:

Option A (Fatty acids and glucose) and Option B (Glucose and galactose) involve sugars, which are not part of the glyceride structure.

Option D (Monoglycerides and glycerol) would be a plausible intermediate product if only one ester bond were hydrolyzed, but complete hydrolysis with lipase would remove both fatty acid groups.

Thus, the best answer is:

Option C – Fatty acids and glycerol.
